quoteFirst: She began to feel anxious about her mother. It was
quoteLast: ; what on earth was she doing? In another five minutes Elizabeth would come with the fly. Her mother had told her to wait in the drawing-room, but she could not wait much longer, she must go and find her.

quoteFirst: The pavement is slippery, rain is beginning to fall--fortunately the Palais Royal is not far off.  At the end of his fourteenth tour round the arcades, Monsieur looks at his watch.

quoteFirst: He wished he might let the Khedive into the secret at once, for he had an opinion of Ismail’s sense of humour; had he not said that very day in the presence of the French Consul, “Shut the window, quick! If the consul sneezes, France will demand compensation!” But Dicky was satisfied that things should be as they were. He looked at the clock--it was
quoteLast: . He rose from the table, and went to the smoking-room. In vain it was sought to draw him into the friendly circles of gossiping idlers and officials.
title: Donovan Pasha, and Some People of Egypt — Complete
author: Parker, Gilbert, 1862-1932

quoteLast: . The orator was glad to see us, thinking his turn would come that day. But I cleaned up within a few dollars of fifteen hundred.

quoteFirst: Paths had been swept in schoolyard between the various class-rooms, but the rest of the broad space lay white and untrodden. David got there while it still wanted
quoteLast: , and hung about with a few friends outside the class-room door till the hour should strike. There was a quiet exchange of small snowballing, furtively delivered, for it was very strictly forbidden in the quadrangle, and David had just lobbed one not bigger than a racquet-ball with extraordinary success, just between the collar and neck of Joynes, who had not the vaguest idea who had done this.
title: David Blaize
author: Benson, E. F. (Edward Frederic), 1867-1940

quoteFirst: The landlord looked at the clock again.
quoteLast: . Diggory Fargus was already twenty-five minutes behind his time. But there was hardly any comfort in the thought.

quoteLast: when their headlights finally fell upon the bridge. As they drew nearer they could hear nothing in the darkness but the thunder of the water.

quoteLast: Simon Smith bade Theresa put on her hat. She said good-night, and again she knew that sense of power as Mr. Smith got out of the chair that dwarfed him, and Neville stopped in his light playing of the piano and gave her his good smile, and Morton looked deeply into her eyes as he opened the door.
title: Yonder
author: Young, E. H. (Emily Hilda), 1880-1949
